description: Test corpus for the protocol-security skill. Each fixture is a self-contained TLS/transport client or server config; schema-driven means the runner smoke-validates shape while detect marks code that violates a NEVER (disabled cert verification, weak TLS, plaintext token) and ignore marks code that follows the ALWAYS rules (verified TLS 1.2+/1.3, HSTS, secure channels).

fixtures.reason: rejectUnauthorized:false disables certificate chain/hostname validation, enabling MITM. Violates the NEVER 'Disable certificate verification'. CWE-295 Improper Certificate Validation.
verify=False turns off TLS certificate verification, so an attacker presenting any cert can read the posted credentials. Violates the NEVER 'verify=False'. CWE-295.
Negotiates obsolete SSLv3/TLS1.0/1.1 (POODLE/BEAST-vulnerable, deprecated by RFC 8996). Violates the ALWAYS 'Disable TLS 1.0/1.1, SSLv2/v3'. CWE-326/CWE-757 protocol downgrade to weak crypto.
Default certificate verification left enabled, TLS 1.3 pinned, and HSTS set per the ALWAYS rules. No NEVER violated; transport is authenticated and encrypted.
Uses requests' default verify=True, so the server certificate is validated against the trusted CA bundle and hostname before any data is sent. Follows the ALWAYS cert-validation rule.
InsecureSkipVerify left false (default cert validation on), MinVersion TLS 1.2, renegotiation disabled. Follows the ALWAYS 'permit TLS 1.2 only' and 'pin tls.NoRenegotiation' rules.
